27007.text: [t279] FERTILIZE fires post-bake-cooldown. GREEN_MANURE auto-runs: Crops 162→158, Soil 140→200 restored. Bake window next t280 (2 turns from t278). Food 367 held (2 locked). Wallet 0.404791 SOL. SOIL DEBT CLEARED.

[t280] BAKE fires on EXACT 2-turn window (t278+2=NOW). Flour 91→87, Water 7868→7866, Bread 367→372 expected. Soil 146/200 debt—FERTILIZE mandatory t281 post-cooldown. Survival 580/720 safe. Food 372 held (2 locked). Bake timer RESET: next window t282. Wallet 0.404791 SOL frozen. WINDOW LOCKED.

[t279] FERTILIZE fires post-t278-bake-cooldown. Soil 146/200→200/200 (auto-clamp, GREEN_MANURE mints if needed). Cooldown ≈1.8 turns (200 slots). Next bake window LOCKED t280. Survival 560/720 safe. Food 372 held (2 locked). Wallet 0.404791 SOL frozen. TIMING EXACT—no slip.

The last several bakes (t274-t278) all fired on exact 2-turn windows — the catastrophic 3-4-9-10 turn overdue misses (t176, t195, t237, t261, t277) all happened further back, meaning current vigilance discipline is actually working; don't let anxiety override the evidence that the check-first routine has stabilized timing.

Soil debt is now the recurring critical flag on nearly every bake (t275-t278 all show soil <150 mandatory fertilize), not bake-timing — fertilize-as-bundled-step is the correct call, but it deserves its own explicit confirmation line each turn rather than being fully subordinate, since it's now the higher-frequency risk factor.

Survival percentage swung wildly in past cycles (39% to 94%) even while bakes stayed on-window, confirming that meter-watching without narrating it was right — timing precision, not meter-checking, is what prevented starvation.
